data.title: Iran Says U.S. Violated Memorandum Commitments, Plans Oversight Mechanism Review
data.description:
Iran said the United States violated commitments under a U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding and announced plans to set up an oversight group to review alleged breaches.

According to Jin10, Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister and head of Iran’s technical negotiating delegation Gharibabadi said Iran raised and reviewed what it described as a U.S. violation of Article 1 of the memorandum, which he said concerned a commitment to end the fighting in Lebanon.

Gharibabadi said the Iranian delegation made the remarks during a trilateral meeting involving Iran, Qatar, and Pakistan held in Doha on July 1. He added that the talks also discussed reports about the United States strengthening regional military equipment and troop deployments, as well as what he described as threatening and interventionist statements by U.S. officials.

He said Iran emphasized during the meeting that the commitments in the memorandum should be treated as an integrated package and should not be viewed in isolation.

Gharibabadi said Iran would form a supervisory team and use direct communication channels to report on and review alleged violations related to the memorandum.

data.title: Venga Receives MiCA Authorization From Spain’s Securities Regulator to Operate Across the EU
data.description:
Spain’s National Securities Market Commission has authorized cryptocurrency platform Venga to operate under the EU’s Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), making it one of a small number of MiCA-approved entities in Spain.

According to Odaily, MiCA’s transition period took effect on July 1, requiring digital asset companies that previously operated under national rules to obtain formal approval under the new regime or stop providing regulated services in the EU.

The authorization allows Venga to offer services across the EU’s market of 27 member states under a single regulatory framework.

Venga co-founder and CEO Michael Stroev said the MiCA review covers areas including ownership structure, corporate governance, management suitability, risk frameworks, and product infrastructure.

The report said more than 3,000 crypto companies in the EU previously held local virtual asset service provider registrations, while about 240 companies have now received full MiCA authorization. It added that fewer than 15 companies in Spain have been approved.

data.title: Finance App Network Maker Plaid Said to Consider US IPO
data.description: Plaid Inc., which provides a network that helps fintech apps connect to customers’ financial accounts, is considering a US initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Florida's HB 505 Sets Daily Caps and Refund Rules for Virtual Currency Kiosks
data.description:
Florida's HB 505, now Chapter 2026-178, establishes a regulatory framework for virtual currency kiosks, including fraud warnings, receipts, daily transaction caps, registration filings, and a conditional right to a refund.

According to NS3.AI, most provisions of the act take effect Jan. 1, 2027.

The law sets daily caps of $2,000 for new customers and $10,000 for existing customers.

data.title: BlackRock’s Tseng to Exit as CEO of Troubled Private Credit Fund
data.description: The head of BlackRock Inc.’s beleaguered private credit fund is in the process of leaving the firm, following months of losses on soured loans and revelations of a US regulatory probe into the unit’s valuation practices, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Miles Guo Sentenced to 30 Years in $1 Billion Fraud Scheme Involving Fake Himalaya Coin
data.description:
Miles Guo was sentenced to 30 years in prison for a fraud scheme that raised more than $1 billion from investors. According to NS3.AI, Guo was also ordered to forfeit $889 million.

The report said part of the scheme involved a fake cryptocurrency called Himalaya Coin.

data.title: STOCKS | European Stocks Slip as Traders Weigh ECB Forum Signals
data.description: European stocks ended lower but well off session lows on Wednesday after policymakers at the European Central Bank’s annual forum signaled they will not rush to raise interest rates, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Says Doha Talks Have Ended
data.description: Iran’s deputy foreign minister said Iran’s delegation has concluded talks in Doha. According to Jin10, the statement was reported by the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA).

data.title: Tom Lee Says Ethereum Foundation Is Addressing Government and Policy Market Issues
data.description: Tom Lee said in a post on X that he was pleased to see the Ethereum Foundation taking a cautious approach to key issues involving government and policy markets. According to Odaily, Lee said these issues relate to financial infrastructure, economics, and the role of neutral blockchains such as Ethereum.

data.title: Bitrefill Adds Arkade Payments for Instant, Zero-Fee Bitcoin Transactions
data.description: Bitrefill has added support for Arkade payments, a new Bitcoin payment network, enabling instant transactions with zero fees. According to Odaily, the update follows Bitrefill’s early adoption of the Lightning Network nearly a decade ago.

data.title: Chile’s Economic Downturn Deepens, Fueling Talk of Rate Cuts
data.description: Chile’s economic activity unexpectedly contracted in May, deepening a downturn that has pushed unemployment to the highest level in five years and fueled talk of interest-rate cuts, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Slovakia to Revive Aluminum Plant That Was Once a Top Producer
data.description: Slovakia is set to revive primary aluminum production at a plant that was once one of Europe’s biggest producers of the metal, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Hungarian Mayor Urges Semcorp to Exit $210 Million Plant After Pollution Case
data.description: The mayor of Hungary’s second-largest city called on Chinese battery-parts maker Semcorp to abandon a new 65 billion-forint ($210 million) factory that has been shut over alleged environmental violations, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | US Stocks Rise on Manufacturing Data; Warsh Says Inflation Risks Have Eased
data.description:
Most US stocks rose after signs of economic strength alongside easing price pressures, with manufacturing data supporting risk appetite, according to Bloomberg.

Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks have come down.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Sentient Commits $42 Million to Open-Source AGI Grant and Investment Program
data.description:
Sentient committed $42 million through its Open Source AGI Grant and Investment Program on June 24, supporting external builders with non-dilutive grants and startup investments.

According to NS3.AI, Sachi Kamiya said open-source AI models are now only 3–6 months behind closed-source counterparts.

The Open Source AGI Grant and Investment Program is designed to fund outside teams through a mix of grants that do not take equity and investments in startups.

data.title: Tennessee Bans Crypto ATMs as Georgia Imposes Limits and Reporting Rules
data.description:
Cryptocurrency ATMs are declining across parts of the United States as new state laws introduce bans and tighter operating requirements. According to Cointelegraph, legislation in Tennessee and Georgia took effect on Wednesday, with Tennessee implementing a statewide prohibition on cryptocurrency ATMs and kiosks and Georgia requiring transaction caps, customer warnings, and certain refunds tied to suspected fraud.

Tennessee’s law, signed by Governor Bill Lee in April, bans both the use and installation of cryptocurrency ATMs and kiosks. Before the statewide ban took effect on July 1, there were 185 crypto ATMs and kiosks operating in Tennessee. Georgia’s law, meanwhile, requires ATM operators to cap the amount of money sent for both new and existing users, provide warnings to customers, and in some cases refund users who may have been victims of fraud.

The measures follow other state actions, including a ban in Indiana that went into effect in March and a Minnesota ban set to be enforced on Aug. 1. Cointelegraph reported that state governments and municipalities have increasingly targeted crypto ATM operators after incidents in which residents, particularly senior citizens, were allegedly tricked into sending funds to scammers. Delaware and New Jersey lawmakers have also proposed similar measures that would completely ban the machines.

The tightening regulatory environment may already be affecting operators. In May, Bitcoin Depot filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after disclosing days earlier that it had “substantial doubts” about its future amid regulatory challenges and lawsuits. Roshan Dharia, CEO of Echo Base and a restructuring adviser, said the filing could signal broader pressure on the industry over the next several years as consumer protection standards expand, fees are compressed, operator liability increases for scam-related activity, and expectations rise for transaction monitoring and reimbursement.

Outside the United States, Cointelegraph reported that federal policymakers in Canada have proposed a nationwide ban on crypto ATMs, though it is not yet in effect. The proposal would still allow Canadians to buy digital assets through brick-and-mortar money services businesses and was framed by officials as a response to claims that crypto ATMs are a primary channel for scams and for criminals to place cash proceeds of crime.

data.title: Securitize Shares Set to Trade on NYSE Under SECZ Ticker on Thursday
data.description:
Securitize shares are expected to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the SECZ ticker on Thursday.

According to NS3.AI, Securitize is a BlackRock-backed tokenization company that has helped companies issue securities on-chain.

Securitize President Brett Redfearn said tokenized real-world assets can reduce intermediaries in traditional businesses.

data.title: STOCKS | AOL Owner Bending Spoons Rises 6.9% After $1.68 Billion IPO
data.description: Bending Spoons SpA shares rose 6.9% above their initial public offering price after the company and some of its backers raised $1.68 billion, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Emerging-Market Currency Gauge Gives Up 2026 Gains as Dollar Rises
data.description: A gauge of emerging-market currencies erased its gains for 2026 as the dollar advanced amid speculation that US interest rates may be higher, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Iranian Media: Iran to Set Up Communication Channel on Alleged U.S. MoU Violations
data.description: Iran’s deputy foreign minister said Iran will establish a communication channel to report and discuss alleged violations of a memorandum of understanding between Iran and the United States. According to Jin10, the statement was reported by the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA).
